questions.prompt: Black box testing is based on:
options: Full knowledge of the source code
Inputs, outputs and requirements, without seeing internals
questions.explanation: Black box tests from the outside — against requirements and observable behaviour, not code.

options: Knowledge of the internal code/structure to exercise paths and branches
questions.explanation: White box uses code knowledge to drive paths, branches and conditions — typically developer-owned.

questions.prompt: Using the API contract and DB schema to design smarter UI tests is:
questions.explanation: Grey box uses partial internal knowledge to design better tests from the outside.

questions.prompt: Most manual testers primarily work in which approach?
options: Black box, often drifting into grey box
questions.explanation: Manual testers mostly test black box and gain grey-box power as they learn the system's internals.

questions.prompt: Inspecting the database to confirm an order row was actually created is an example of:
questions.explanation: Peeking at the DB to verify a UI action is grey-box testing — partial internal knowledge applied from outside.
